---
title: "Introduction to Physical AI"
subtitle: "Course Website Template"
---

Instructor: Yonghyeon Lee

## Course Overview
This course introduces the core ideas behind Physical AI, covering how embodied
systems sense, move, reason, plan, learn, and interact with the physical world.
The lectures connect classical robotics foundations with modern data-driven
learning methods and discuss practical applications and societal impact.

## Lecture Schedule

| Week | Lecture | Notes | Slides |
|---|---|---|---|
| Week 1 | Lecture 01: Introduction: What is Physical AI? | [Notes](notes/what_is_physical_ai.qmd) | [Slides](https://1drv.ms/p/c/831745eefddf18d9/IQDcJe5nNr1aT55wQnANUypHAR9lrb_BRc5bE60a1k5iy3M?e=qC3FmL) |
| Week 2 | Lecture 02: Anatomy of a Physical AI System |  |  |
| Week 3 | Lecture 03: Sensors: How Robots Perceive the World |  |  |
| Week 4 | Lecture 04: Actuators: How Robots Move |  |  |
| Week 5 | Lecture 05: Robot Bodies and Embodiment |  |  |
| Week 6 | Lecture 06: Basic Geometry and Motion Representation |  |  |
| Week 7 | Lecture 07: Perception and World Understanding |  |  |
| Week 8 | Lecture 08: From Goals to Actions: Planning and Policy |  |  |
| Week 9 | Lecture 09: Feedback and Control |  |  |
| Weeks 10-11 | Lecture 10: Learning for Physical AI |  |  |
| Week 12 | Lecture 11: Physics for Physical AI |  |  |
| Week 13 | Lecture 12: Integrating Data and Physics |  |  |
| Week 14 | Lecture 13: Applications and Societal Impact |  |  |
| Weeks 15-16 | Lecture 14: Final Presentations / Future of Physical AI |  |  |

Full notes index: [Lecture Notes](notes/index.qmd)<br>
Full slides index: [Lecture Slides](slides/index.qmd)
